title: callicarpa stapfii
intro: Callicarpa stapfii, also known as the purple plumier or purple fruit callicarpa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Callicarpa stapfii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Callicarpa stapfii typically involve boiling the plant's leaves and roots in water, which can then be used as a tea or applied topically for its medicinal properties.
